notes: All transfer applicants must satisfy University of California admissions eligibility requirements and campus admission selection criteria. All admission requirements must be completed by the end of spring prior to transfer. For more information on UC admissions eligibility requirements and admission to UC Santa Cruz, please visit the Admissions website . The UC Santa Cruz Transfer Admission Guarantee (TAG) is a formal agreement that ensures your fall admission to your chosen major, provided you are transferring from a California Community College and meet specific requirements. Visit the UC Santa Cruz TAG webpage for more information.

Transfer students can readily finish all of the Jewish Studies major requirements within two years, even if they haven't completed any preparatory coursework prior to transferring. Some background in history and in literary analysis and interpretation is desirable, but not essential. Since this is an interdisciplinary major, preparatory work in art history, philosophy, or politics, depending on the student's interests, may also be useful. PREPARATION FOR THE MAJOR Although not required for admission, transfer students are encouraged to complete the following courses, or their articulated equivalents, prior to transferring: ONE lower-division core course in Jewish history or literature (section A below) ONE lower-division Holocaust core course (section B below) ONE lower-division elective (section C below)* *May be an additional core or language course Refer to the "Recommended Preparation" section below for lists of courses approved to fulfill these requirements. LANGUAGE REQUIREMENT The Jewish Studies B.A. requires three quarters of lower-division instruction (or equivalent) in a Jewish language. Transfer students with prior knowledge of Hebrew or Yiddish are advised to complete a language placement exam to determine if they have satisfied any of the elementary language course requirements. Students are also encouraged to complete the Intersegmental General Education Transfer Curriculum (IGETC) , or all UC Santa Cruz general education requirements before matriculation to ensure timely graduation. For more information on incorporating IGETC into your academic plan, please consult with your community college counselor. ﻿Please visit the Jewish Studies website to learn more about this major.

The UC Santa Cruz Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate (IB), and A-Level examination credit policies may be found in the UC Santa Cruz General Catalog . AP, IBH, and A-level exams may or may not satisfy course requirements, depending on the specific exam.

This is a non-screening major: completion of specific major coursework is not required for admission. Although not required for admission, students are strongly encouraged to complete as many lower-division preparatory courses as possible from among those listed in the “Recommended Preparation” section before transferring to UC Santa Cruz. Doing so will help you make timely progress toward degree completion. Additionally, completing the Intersegmental General Education Transfer Curriculum (IGETC) is encouraged, especially for non-screening majors in the Humanities, Arts, and Social Sciences Divisions. While not required for admission, IGETC completion will facilitate timely graduation. For more information on incorporating IGETC into your academic plan, please consult with your community college counselor.
